subject: [PATCH v6 08/10] dax/kmem: extract hotplug/hotremove helper functions

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch refactors the kmem probe and remove functions by extracting initialization, cleanup, hotplug, and hot-remove logic into separate helper functions. The helpers enable future extensions to support more granular control over memory hotplug operations. The patch splits hotplug/hotunplug and init/cleanup to make resources available for hot-add, allowing re-use of the hotunplug code without destroying underlying resources.

reviews.author: Dave Jiang
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer Dave Jiang pointed out a minor inconsistency in comment wording, suggesting 'later' be used instead of 'below', as the function flow has changed.
reviews.raw_body:
'later' instead of 'below'? Comment reflects previous function flow that is no longer the case.

DJ

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that a fix is needed and promises to address it in a future revision, specifically mentioning 'respin'.
reviews.raw_body:
ack. will pick it up on the respin

~Gregory

2026-07-09.patch_summary: This patch refactors the kmem probe and remove functions by extracting initialization, cleanup, hotplug, and hot-remove logic into separate helper functions. The helpers enable future extensions to support more granular control over memory hotplug operations. This is a pure refactoring with no functional change, allowing for re-use of hotunplug code without destroying underlying resources.
